sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: FC St. Pauli are struggling, sitting at 16th in the Bundesliga with only 7 points and a poor run of form in their last 5 matches (1 draw, 4 losses). In contrast, 1. FC Union Berlin, positioned 12th with 12 points, show better resilience with an unbeaten streak of 3 matches (1 win, 2 draws). Union's recent performances indicate a stronger momentum heading into this clash.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: The last two encounters between these teams resulted in one win each, with St. Pauli securing a convincing 3-0 victory at home in January 2025, while Union Berlin edged a 1-0 win in August 2024. Historically, matches between these sides have been competitive, with 5 of the last 6 featuring over 2.5 goals, suggesting a high likelihood of goals in this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Tactics
sections.text: For St. Pauli, forward Danel Sinani (6.86 rating, 2 goals) will be key in breaking Union’s defense, while their high pressing (2nd in penalty area entries) could challenge Union’s backline. Union Berlin’s Danilho Doekhi (7.21 rating, 4 goals) is in exceptional form, posing a threat from set pieces. Union’s ability to win aerial duels (evident from their stats) could exploit St. Pauli’s weakness in the air (18th in aerial duels won).

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: St. Pauli’s defense has been leaky, conceding 20 goals this season (13th worst), and they’ve failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 10 matches. Union Berlin, despite conceding 17 goals, have shown better attacking output with 13 goals scored compared to St. Pauli’s 9. Union’s recent trend of high corner counts (over 10.5 in 4 of last 5) aligns with their attacking intent.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Union Berlin’s superior form and St. Pauli’s defensive struggles, the Sofascore prediction of an away win (42%) seems plausible over a home win (33%) or draw (25%). Union’s current streak and key players like Doekhi give them an edge, though St. Pauli’s home advantage at Millerntor-Stadion could make it a close contest. Expect a tactical battle with goals likely on both sides.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Millerntor-Stadion saw 1. FC Union Berlin take a narrow lead over FC St. Pauli with a late goal from Rani Khedira in the 44th minute. Union Berlin displayed a more clinical approach, capitalizing on their opportunities while St. Pauli struggled to create meaningful chances. Despite a balanced possession share, Union Berlin dominated key attacking areas, showing more intent in the final third. St. Pauli, on the other hand, found it difficult to penetrate Union Berlin’s defense, resulting in a frustrating half for the home side.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Union Berlin showcased a stronger attacking presence in the first half, with significantly more entries into the final third and penalty area compared to St. Pauli. Their ability to win duels, especially in the air, allowed them to maintain control during critical moments. St. Pauli, while holding their own in terms of possession, lacked the cutting edge needed to test Union Berlin’s defense. The home team’s defensive efforts, particularly from players like Karol Mets with notable clearances and interceptions, kept them in the game, but their inability to convert possession into dangerous opportunities was evident.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half began with St. Pauli showing early intent, pushing forward with a series of attacking spells in the opening minutes. However, momentum shifted toward Union Berlin as the half progressed, with the away side gaining control and sustaining pressure through consistent attacking waves. St. Pauli had brief moments of resurgence, trying to build play in the opponent’s half, but Union Berlin’s dominance in the latter stages of the half was clear, culminating in their well-timed goal just before the break. The away team’s persistent forward momentum late in the half highlighted their attacking intent.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and moments
halftimeSections.text: Rani Khedira was the standout performer for Union Berlin, not only scoring the decisive goal but also contributing significantly in midfield with his interceptions and overall presence. For St. Pauli, players like Karol Mets and Hauke Wahl were crucial in defense, working hard to limit Union Berlin’s chances. Andreas Hountondji showed some creativity for the home side with a key pass, but it wasn’t enough to break through. The defining moment of the half came late, as Union Berlin capitalized on a defensive lapse from St. Pauli to secure the lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, St. Pauli will need to regroup and find a way to create more threatening opportunities if they hope to equalize. The home crowd at Millerntor-Stadion could play a significant role in lifting their spirits, but they must improve their final third execution. Union Berlin, buoyed by their first-half performance, will likely aim to maintain their defensive solidity while seeking to exploit counter-attacking chances. St. Pauli may push harder, potentially opening up spaces for Union Berlin to capitalize on. A key battle will be in midfield, where controlling the tempo could decide the outcome.
